title: Cleaner, less crowded dashboard
changes: Trimmed the top stat cards from seven down to the four that matter — Vehicles, Out on jobs, Service overdue, Service soon.
Service due and Out on jobsites now sit side by side instead of stacked, so there's far less scrolling.
The four CSV export buttons are now one tidy 'Export' menu in the top bar.
The vehicles table is narrower — make, model, and plate now sit neatly under the vehicle name.

title: Easier to learn — welcome guide & built-in help
changes: New: a first-run welcome guide that walks new users through adding vehicles, printing QR codes, and running check-ins.
New: a built-in Help guide — click '? Help' in the top bar for how-to topics covering every feature.
Friendlier empty states — an empty Vehicles list now prompts you to add your first vehicle instead of showing a blank space.
Inline hints and tooltips added across the dashboard so panels and buttons explain themselves.

title: First versioned release
changes: Fleet dashboard — a live, dark-themed control panel listing every vehicle with its status, location, odometer, and next service due.

Vehicle check-ins — employees complete an enforced 7-step inspection checklist (odometer, oil, tire rotation, wash, fluids, tire condition, lights); every check-in is logged with full per-vehicle history.
Service schedule — projects when each vehicle next needs an oil change, tire rotation, wash, and inspection, flagging anything overdue or due soon.
Printable QR codes — every vehicle gets a QR code (single card or a full sheet) that opens its mobile service page when scanned.
Damage photos — upload and review photos of vehicle damage from the dashboard or a phone.
Jobsite dispatch — mark vehicles out on a job and back at the business, with a live 'out on jobsites' view and trip history.
Spreadsheet reports — one-click CSV exports for vehicles, check-ins, the service schedule, and jobsite trips.
PIN-protected phone access — devices on the local network enter a shared PIN; the admin dashboard on the host Mac needs no login.
One-line installer — a curl-based install that sets FleetFinder up as a background service on macOS.
agent-hub integration — FleetFinder is discovered as a sibling hub so agents can read and update the fleet.
